description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with ruby:2.4 as the default image and docker:latest (with docker:dind service) for Docker‑related jobs. Define a global before_script that runs `ruby -v`, `which ruby`, `gem install bundler --no-ri --no-rdoc`, and `bundle install --jobs $(nproc) "${FLAGS[@]}"`. Add an rspec job in the test stage that uses a cache of the `vendor` directory and executes `bundle exec rspec -f d -c -b`. Add a rubocop job (also in test) that simply runs the job name variable `$CI_JOB_NAME`. Add a test_dockerfile job in the test stage that, except on the master branch, runs a Docker‑specific before_script (`eval $(grep -e "^version: " config.yaml | sed -e "s/version: /BUNDLE_VERSION=/")`, `export DOCKER_REPOSITORY="gitlab/cog"`, `export TAG="$DOCKER_REPOSITORY:$BUNDLE_VERSION"`, `echo "TAG VERSION $TAG"`, `docker build -t "$TAG" .`) and then builds the image with `docker build ./`, using the docker:dind service and `DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay`. Finally, add a deploy_dockerfile job in the deploy stage that runs only on the master branch, reuses the same Docker before_script, logs into Docker Hub with `docker login -u "$DOCKER_HUB_USERNAME" -p "$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD"`, pushes the built tag with `docker push "$TAG"`, tags it as latest with `docker tag "$TAG" "$DOCKER_REPOSITORY:latest"` and pushes the latest tag, also using docker:dind and the overlay driver.
